title: Associate AI or ML Engineer
description:
Requisition Number: 2333074 Optum is a global organization
that delivers care, aided by technology to help millions of
people live healthier lives. The work you do with our team
will directly improve health outcomes by connecting people
with the care, pharmacy benefits, data and resources they
need to feel their best. Here, you will find a culture
guided by inclusion, talented peers, comprehensive benefits
and career development opportunities. Come make an impact on
the communities we serve as you help us advance health
optimization on a global scale. Join us to start Caring.
Connecting. Growing together. Primary Responsibilities:

- Develop AI/ML solutions for ECS Big Bets and AI 10.0
  initiatives, focusing on automation of clinical workflows
  and operational efficiency
- Build and integrate models for tasks like Touchless Intake,
  Scheduling, Voice AI, and Prior Authorization
- Implement unit testing, code coverage, and test automation
  to ensure quality and reliability
- Collaborate with global engineering teams to deliver secure,
  scalable, and compliant solutions
- Work on front-end and back-end components using React,
  Next.js, and PostgreSQL
- Optimize data pipelines and model performance for production
  environments
- Stay current with AI technologies, including LLMs, prompt
  engineering, and multi-agentic architectures
- Comply with the terms and conditions of the employment
  contract, company policies and procedures, and any and all
  directives (such as, but not limited to, transfer and/or
  re-assignment to different work locations, change in teams
  and/or work shifts, policies in regards to flexibility of
  work benefits and/or work environment, alternative work
  arrangements, and other decisions that may arise due to
  the changing business environment). The Company may adopt,
  vary or rescind these policies and directives in its
  absolute discretion and without any limitation (implied or
  otherwise) on its ability to do so

Required Qualifications:

- Software development experience, with exposure to AI/ML
  projects
- Knowledge of unit testing and test automation tools (e.g.,
  Jest, PyTest)
- Understanding of AI/ML concepts and ability to work with
  pre-trained models
- Basic understanding of cloud platforms (Azure/AWS) and
  containerization (Docker/Kubernetes)
- Familiarity with CI/CD pipelines and deployment processes
- Exposure to Large Language Models (LLMs) and prompt
  engineering (hands-on experience preferred)
- Basic proficiency in Python and familiarity with React,
  Next.js, and PostgreSQL
- Willingness to learn LLM gateways, MCP, and agentic/multi-
  agentic solutions
- Proven solid problem-solving skills and ability to work in a
  collaborative team environment

Preferred Qualification:

- Interest in healthcare workflows and AI observability tools
  like Arize AX

At UnitedHealth Group, our mission is to help people live
healthier lives and make the health system work better for
everyone. We believe everyone-of every race, gender,
sexuality, age, location and income-deserves the opportunity
to live their healthiest life. Today, however, there are
still far too many barriers to good health which are
disproportionately experienced by people of color,
historically marginalized groups and those with lower
incomes. We are committed to mitigating our impact on the
environment and enabling and delivering equitable care that
addresses health disparities and improves health outcomes -
an enterprise priority reflected in our mission.

ai_classification_reasoning:
The role involves building AI/ML solutions including LLMs,
prompt engineering, and multi-agentic architectures. The
engineer will build and integrate models for clinical
workflows like Touchless Intake, Voice AI, and Prior
Authorization, which is hands-on work with AI systems rather
than just supporting infrastructure.
